name: Scaphoid Fracture
description: Fracture of the scaphoid bone from FOOSH.
red_flags: Pain with axial loading of thumb
key_findings: Radial-sided wrist pain after trauma.
diagnostic_tests: MRI if X-ray negative but symptoms persist
referral_criteria: Refer to ortho/ED if snuffbox tenderness is present following FOOSH.

name: Peripheral Artery Disease (PAD)
description: Atherosclerotic narrowing of peripheral arteries, causing ischemia.
red_flags: Rest pain suggesting critical limb ischemia
key_findings: Intermittent claudication (cramping pain in calf with consistently reproducible exertion, improving with rest).
referral_criteria: Refer to vascular specialist if ABI < 0.9 or evidence of critical limb ischemia.

name: Deep Vein Thrombosis (DVT)
description: Formation of a blood clot in a deep vein, most commonly the calf or thigh.
red_flags: Chest pain or shortness of breath (Pulmonary Embolism)
key_findings: Unilateral leg swelling, warmth, redness, and deep calf pain.
referral_criteria: IMMEDIATE Emergency Department referral if suspected.

name: Cubital Tunnel Syndrome
description: Ulnar nerve compression at the elbow.
key_findings: Numbness/tingling in pinky and medial half of ring finger, elbow pain, worsened by elbow flexion.
referral_criteria: Refer to hand surgeon if constant numbness or intrinsic muscle weakness is present.

name: Carpal Tunnel Syndrome
description: Compression neuropathic condition of the median nerve at the wrist.
key_findings: Numbness/tingling in the thumb, index, middle, and half of ring finger. Night pain. Dropping objects.
referral_criteria: Refer for surgical decompression if thenar atrophy or severe EMG findings are present.

name: Lumbar Spinal Stenosis
description: Narrowing of the spinal canal or intervertebral foramina, resulting in neurogenic claudication.
key_findings: Bilateral leg pain/heaviness with walking or standing, relieved by sitting or lumbar flexion (shopping cart sign).
referral_criteria: Refer to neuro/ortho spine if conservative management fails after 6-12 weeks.

name: Lumbar Disc Herniation (HNP)
description: Extrusion of the nucleus pulposus through the annulus fibrosus, frequently compressing a nerve root.
key_findings: Unilateral radiating leg pain (sciatica) below the knee, worse with flexion, better with extension.
referral_criteria: Immediate referral to ED for Cauda Equina symptoms or rapid neurological decline.

name: Cervical Radiculopathy
description: Nerve root compression or irritation causing pain, sensory, and motor deficits in the upper extremity matching a dermatomal/myotomal pattern.
key_findings: Unilateral pattern, (+) Spurling's, relief with distraction.
referral_criteria: Refer for MRI/Surgical consult if progressive motor deficits or myelopathy signs are present.

name: Myocardial Infarction (Referred Pain)
description: Ischemia to the heart muscle that can masquerade as musculoskeletal chest, shoulder, or jaw pain.
red_flags: Pain not altered by physical movement or position
key_findings: Vitals abnormal, patient appears unwell, reproduction of symptoms with cardiovascular demand but not MSK testing.
referral_criteria: Immediate Emergency Medical Services (EMS) activation.

name: Temporomandibular Joint Dysfunction (TMD)
description: Pain and dysfunction of the muscles of mastication and the temporomandibular joint.
key_findings: Clicking/popping in jaw, limited mouth opening, masseter/temporalis tenderness, headaches.
referral_criteria: Refer to dentist if occlusal bite issues are primary, or neurologist if cranial nerve signs.

name: Thoracic Outlet Syndrome (TOS)
description: Compression of the neurovascular bundle (brachial plexus, subclavian artery/vein) crossing the thoracic outlet.
red_flags: Sudden acute swelling and cyanosis of the arm (Venous TOS)
key_findings: Pain, tingling, and heaviness in the arm, especially with overhead activities. Global non-dermatomal numbness.
referral_criteria: Vascular TOS (Arterial/Venous) requires immediate surgical vascular referral. Neurogenic TOS starts with PT.

red_flags: Sudden acute swelling and cyanosis of the arm (Venous TOS)
key_findings: Pain, tingling, and heaviness in the arm, especially with overhead activities. Global non-dermatomal numbness.
referral_criteria: Vascular TOS (Arterial/Venous) requires immediate surgical vascular referral. Neurogenic TOS starts with PT.

name: Cervical Radiculopathy (Variant 73)
description: Nerve root compression or irritation causing pain, sensory, and motor deficits in the upper extremity matching a dermatomal/myotomal pattern.
key_findings: Unilateral pattern, (+) Spurling's, relief with distraction.
referral_criteria: Refer for MRI/Surgical consult if progressive motor deficits or myelopathy signs are present.

name: Myocardial Infarction (Referred Pain) (Variant 73)
description: Ischemia to the heart muscle that can masquerade as musculoskeletal chest, shoulder, or jaw pain.
red_flags: Pain not altered by physical movement or position
key_findings: Vitals abnormal, patient appears unwell, reproduction of symptoms with cardiovascular demand but not MSK testing.
referral_criteria: Immediate Emergency Medical Services (EMS) activation.

name: Temporomandibular Joint Dysfunction (TMD) (Variant 73)
description: Pain and dysfunction of the muscles of mastication and the temporomandibular joint.
key_findings: Clicking/popping in jaw, limited mouth opening, masseter/temporalis tenderness, headaches.
referral_criteria: Refer to dentist if occlusal bite issues are primary, or neurologist if cranial nerve signs.

red_flags: Pain not altered by physical movement or position
key_findings: Vitals abnormal, patient appears unwell, reproduction of symptoms with cardiovascular demand but not MSK testing.
referral_criteria: Immediate Emergency Medical Services (EMS) activation.

name: Temporomandibular Joint Dysfunction (TMD) (Variant 91)
description: Pain and dysfunction of the muscles of mastication and the temporomandibular joint.
key_findings: Clicking/popping in jaw, limited mouth opening, masseter/temporalis tenderness, headaches.
referral_criteria: Refer to dentist if occlusal bite issues are primary, or neurologist if cranial nerve signs.

name: Thoracic Outlet Syndrome (TOS) (Variant 91)
description: Compression of the neurovascular bundle (brachial plexus, subclavian artery/vein) crossing the thoracic outlet.
red_flags: Sudden acute swelling and cyanosis of the arm (Venous TOS)
key_findings: Pain, tingling, and heaviness in the arm, especially with overhead activities. Global non-dermatomal numbness.
referral_criteria: Vascular TOS (Arterial/Venous) requires immediate surgical vascular referral. Neurogenic TOS starts with PT.

name: Cervical Radiculopathy (Variant 92)
description: Nerve root compression or irritation causing pain, sensory, and motor deficits in the upper extremity matching a dermatomal/myotomal pattern.
key_findings: Unilateral pattern, (+) Spurling's, relief with distraction.
referral_criteria: Refer for MRI/Surgical consult if progressive motor deficits or myelopathy signs are present.

key_findings: Vitals abnormal, patient appears unwell, reproduction of symptoms with cardiovascular demand but not MSK testing.
referral_criteria: Immediate Emergency Medical Services (EMS) activation.

name: Temporomandibular Joint Dysfunction (TMD) (Variant 202)
description: Pain and dysfunction of the muscles of mastication and the temporomandibular joint.
key_findings: Clicking/popping in jaw, limited mouth opening, masseter/temporalis tenderness, headaches.
referral_criteria: Refer to dentist if occlusal bite issues are primary, or neurologist if cranial nerve signs.

name: Thoracic Outlet Syndrome (TOS) (Variant 202)
description: Compression of the neurovascular bundle (brachial plexus, subclavian artery/vein) crossing the thoracic outlet.
red_flags: Sudden acute swelling and cyanosis of the arm (Venous TOS)
key_findings: Pain, tingling, and heaviness in the arm, especially with overhead activities. Global non-dermatomal numbness.
referral_criteria: Vascular TOS (Arterial/Venous) requires immediate surgical vascular referral. Neurogenic TOS starts with PT.

name: Cervical Radiculopathy (Variant 203)
description: Nerve root compression or irritation causing pain, sensory, and motor deficits in the upper extremity matching a dermatomal/myotomal pattern.
key_findings: Unilateral pattern, (+) Spurling's, relief with distraction.
referral_criteria: Refer for MRI/Surgical consult if progressive motor deficits or myelopathy signs are present.
